prompt: We're building a data integration middleware platform that connects enterprise systems - think Salesforce, Snowflake, Databricks, SAP - with a no-code visual builder and AI-powered data mapping. Series B, $30M raised. Target: data engineering teams and IT leaders at enterprises with 50+ SaaS tools. Should feel: technical but accessible, reliable, enterprise-grade. The core value prop is interoperability - we're the connective tissue. Competitors: MuleSoft, Boomi, Fivetran, Airbyte, Workato, Tray.io. Name needs to work in 'Powered by [X]' and '[X] for Salesforce' integration grammar. Developer audience skews technical - they'll judge us on the name.

assertions.text: At least 6 of 11 naming categories attempted
Integration Topology Naming applied (connection, flow, aggregation vocabulary)
Buyer Committee test applied
Sales Conversation Fit templates tested
Developer/Technical Credibility Signaling applied
Partner/Channel Naming Grammar tested ('[X] for Salesforce', 'Powered by [X]')
Stack test run (name alongside Salesforce, Snowflake, Databricks, AWS)
Ownability-first compounds generated in parallel track
Tech Branding Patterns applied
Prefix x Suffix Matrix used for systematic combinatorial generation
Obscure Real Word Mining from specialized domains (textile/maritime/architecture)
Morphological Analysis (Zwicky) applied for systematic coverage
Each riff labeled with which technique produced it
10+ NEW UNIQUE riffs per direction (duplicates of existing candidates don't count)
Systematic Affix Exploration applied (roots in prefix AND suffix positions)
B2B riffing checklist used (SCAMPER, compound creation, creative respelling, tech branding)
Hard gate checks displayed before generation menu
Inline conflict flagging for known conflicts (⚠️ emoji or text flag — substance over format)
At least 40 unique candidates generated
Acronym Resilience tested
Internal-Champion Forwardability considered
Technique toolkit offered at checkpoint with applied/unapplied status and user selection option
